task_completion.description: Open the Panoply application and load the air.mon.ltm.nc dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the Panoply GUI open with the 'air.mon.ltm.nc' dataset loaded in the sources/datasets panel.

task_completion.description: Create and save a 1D zonal mean line plot for January.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a 1D line plot in Panoply for the 'air' variable specifically for January (Time 1), and evidence of it being saved as 'zonal_temp_jan.png' in /home/ga/Documents/ThermalDynamics.

task_completion.description: Create and save a 1D zonal mean line plot for July.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a 1D line plot in Panoply for the 'air' variable specifically for July (Time 7), and evidence of it being saved as 'zonal_temp_jul.png' in /home/ga/Documents/ThermalDynamics.

task_completion.description: Document the January Equator-to-Pole temperature gradient magnitude in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of a text editor showing 'gradient_report.txt' containing the January temperature gradient value (approximately 53.76°C).

task_completion.description: Document the July Equator-to-Pole temperature gradient magnitude in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of a text editor showing 'gradient_report.txt' containing the July temperature gradient value (approximately 23.26°C).

task_completion.description: Identify which month features the stronger jet stream in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the 'gradient_report.txt' explicitly stating that January has the stronger jet stream (or stronger thermal gradient leading to a stronger jet stream).

integrity.description: The agent used the Panoply graphical interface to generate the plots.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Panoply application windows being interacted with to create the 1D line plots, rather than using terminal scripts (e.g., Python/matplotlib) to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: The agent manually created the text report using a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a text editor (like gedit, nano, or vim) open with the gradient report being typed or edited, rather than being generated entirely via a bash echo or python script.

integrity.description: The agent extracted the temperature values by interacting with the data in Panoply.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent hovering over or clicking on the Panoply plots/arrays to read the Equator and Pole temperatures before writing them in the report.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset is 'air.mon.ltm.nc' containing the 'air' variable. The expected January Equator-to-North-Pole temperature gradient is approximately 53.76°C. The expected July Equator-to-North-Pole temperature gradient is approximately 23.26°C. Based on the Thermal Wind relationship, January features the stronger thermal gradient and therefore the stronger jet stream. The expected output files are 'zonal_temp_jan.png', 'zonal_temp_jul.png', and 'gradient_report.txt' located in '/home/ga/Documents/ThermalDynamics'.
